question_text: After proving two triangles congruent, which statement can be justified next?
option_a: Corresponding parts of congruent triangles are equal
option_b: The triangles must also be similar with scale factor not equal to \(1\)
option_c: Every angle in both triangles is \(90^\circ\)
option_d: All six sides of the figure are equal
explanation: Once congruence is established, corresponding parts are equal.
